03 — People

Rig Careers

Every position on a rig, what it pays, and how to get there. No experience required to start — just the willingness to learn from the ground up.

Overview

Rig Hierarchy

Every person on a rig has a specific function. Drilling crews operate in 8- or 12-hour tours (shifts), with clear lines of authority from floorhand to company man.

PositionResponsibilitiesPay Range (US)Experience
Company ManClient Rep
On-site representative of the oil company funding operations. Final authority on well program decisions.
$150K–$300K
15+ yrs
ToolpusherRig Supervisor
Overall supervisor of rig contractor's operations. Manages all crews, tools, and equipment.
$90K–$180K
10–15 yrs
DrillerCrew Supervisor
Controls drilling from the console. Monitors WOB, RPM, torque, pump pressure. Supervises the crew.
$70K–$120K
5–8 yrs
DerrickmanSpecialist
Works the monkey board 90–100 ft up guiding pipe stands. Operates mud pumps and monitors pit volumes.
$55K–$90K
3–5 yrs
RoughneckRig Floor Crew
Makes up/breaks out pipe connections. Operates the catwalk. The backbone of drilling operations.
$45K–$70K
1–3 yrs
RoustaboutEntry Level
General labor: painting, cleaning, moving supplies, assisting crane operations. Entry point for most careers.
$38K–$55K
No exp required

© 2026 RigFloorHQ. Educational and reference content only. Always follow your company's approved procedures, regulatory requirements, and the instructions of your certified well control supervisor. Well control decisions must never be made solely from reference materials.
